樂評《願榮光歸香港》
Criticism in Music - Glory to Hong Kong
(English below)

上次簡略介紹了一些可用的工具來作批評文學，現在當然是真係寫作一篇批評文學了。我們做的是文化工作，當然要在文化實力上把關。

1. 歌詞
這歌詞和我們日常談話的語言是很不同的，也沒有什麼詩意。歌詞開初使用了很多反問，而反問句基本上全部都沒有回答，這是近似於控訴式的傷痕文學字詞。但這種傷痕卻又不是很深的傷痕，近似於撞傷撞瘀，而不是流血如注。沒呼天搶地，只道悲暗愁煩，也沒有表達什麼希望，近似於以死控訴的心境。

2. 旋律
這絕對不是如歌的旋律，每一句句子都很難做出什麼連貫句，每一句也像斬件式的，一塊叉燒斬開三件的模樣。

如果是先曲後詞，這曲一開始便將人說話的句子分拆開，每一下也像是「擢一擢」的感覺，有如開船進大海，不斷被海浪打斷氣息。這樣的音樂是不論填上什麼語言也會令人覺得難唱。

如果是先詞後曲，這詞寫得也很奇怪。唐詩宋詞也沒有以兩三字為一句，標點符號佔了全文三分一篇幅的寫法。

3. 旋律與歌詞的配合
基本上歌詞和旋律是沒有關連的。「淚再流」ti-do-la，「亦憤恨」mi-fa-mi，都是停在穩定而大調色彩豐富的地方；再下句「拒沉默」居然是高音的 fa-mi-re，而「吶喊聲響透」是 mi-fa-sol-mi-do 向下走。基本上旋律的方向和文字是幾乎相反。

而如果你真的要說這是軍歌式風格的，至少旋律不要向下走，不要每一句句尾都是向下的。

4. 拍子
基本上由一開始到後半首的忽然變動為止，都是一個基本的拍子：附點一拍、半拍、附點一拍、半拍。完全沒有變化。然後忽然出現了一些四字詞用了很忽然出現的東西，是和整首音樂沒有主題連貫的。

5. 節奏
每一個小節的正拍沒有什麼強烈的感覺，沒有造出方向感。每一句也是平伏的進行。

6. 大細聲變化
完全沒有。

7. 配器法
歌詞寫了很多的「吶喊」、「響透」、「號角聲」，但基本上銅管沒有一個明顯的角色。用作演奏和聲的部份也只是有中低音域，沒有就每一件樂器的特色來給予他們不同的音域來突顯優勢。背後偶然聽到的小鼓聲也是不明所以，沒有人用小鼓得來又用得這麼鬼祟的。要在和聲低聲部增加強度的話，其實是適合使用多一點的長號吹長音，或者使用大提琴和低音大提琴拉奏短促和重覆的音，而不是要求全隊樂團以單粒單粒音的形式去「墊底」。

8. 功能和聲
這音樂沒有其他的音樂語言，他是選擇了功性和聲，而且是最老土的一款。但不要緊，問題是，使用得是否鬆張有度？不，基本上每一句也是同一功能：IV-V-I，沒有變化。每一句都以IV開首，轉入V，再回來I。這種... 是刻意的「按章式」功能和聲。是刻意製造沉悶的感覺才會用的。其實當你認為社運應該如流水，為何不找泛調性來用用？或者是近似於傳統粵樂中的五聲音階的調式音樂？

而且我不明白為何要使用大調得來，旋律是向下，而且是用深沉的歌詞和音樂內容。

其實表達失望或「墜落徬徨」時，可以使用小調，主力使用 iv -> i ，甚至是 iv -> vii° -> i 這種強烈的推進。當臨尾談到希望是，再轉回大調，使用 I -> IV -> V -> vi 再 I -> IV -> V -> I 等。

9. 轉調
完全無轉調。其實你隻歌明顯係分開兩段，有不同的主題、有不同的拍子、有不同的內容，是應該使用不同的調。

10. 填詞合音
還算可以。

總結 (1/10)，十項中只有一項是花過精神和心血的。

第1至9項也是下苦功不足，基本上這種作品是起稿的第一版本，多數拿到作曲課的老師面前也令人自己害羞的。

作曲的人，和自己所作的曲，是很親切和微妙的關係。你作為一個作曲家，你要和自己的音樂好好相處一下，要花點時間心血去改進。作好出來，你既然有連登的朋友，就應該好好請教一下他們，來回至少三五個版本，再慢慢改進，做得至少合符一個作曲家的心血，才會放出來教人演奏傳播。

這首音樂，是有潛質的，可惜完全沒有雕琢、沒有花心血去改進，像是老師要求交功課便隨便完成交貨似的，作曲家沒有和音樂相處得很久似的，也見不到有向他的連登網友們發問，是一種低廉、低努力的創作。

Last, we have talked about a few instruments to perform Music Criticism. Let's practice it. We are doing cultural works, and of course, we have to put some work in terms of culture.

1. Lyrics
The lyrics are so different to our daily speech. It's also not very poetic. At the beginning, there have been many rhetorical questions, and they are never answered. The texts sound like a complaint with a scar. But it is not a deep scar, something more like a blunt injury instead of spilling blood. No outcry of desperation, just gloomy and troubled. Also not much hope. It's almost like a death accusation.

2. Melody
It is absolutely not "Cantabile". Every sentence is very disconnected like bricks laying out, very choppy.

If the music comes first and the lyrics filled later, then the music is already trying to chop up a person's speech. Every line feels like being rocked in a boat at high sea. Whichever language being filled into the same piece of music will make it difficult to sing at all.

If the text comes first, then the texts are themselves very interesting. The Tang poetry and Song Ci never have something as choppy as 2 to 3 words a segregation. It's almost like punctuation marks are occupying 1/3 of the whole text.

3. Compatibility of melody and text
Basically the text and the melody are not related to each other. "Lui-Joi-Lau" (to be tearful again) is ti-do-la, and "Yik-Fan-Han" (also angry and vengeful) is mi-fa-mi. They are both stopped at a very strong colour of the Major Key and very stable. The next "Kui-Cham-Mak" (refuse to be silent) is however at a high pitch fa-mi-re, while "Lab-ham-sing-heung-tau" (sound of outcry sounding thoroughly) is at mi-fa-sol-mi-do, both going downwards. The text's meaning or direction is almost going in opposite to the melody.

If this piece of music is aimed at being a March, then at least the melody shouldn't be always going downwards.

4. Beats
From the beginning up until the sudden changes, it's always the same: dotted beat, half beat, dotted beat and half beat. No variety. And then suddenly near the end, the sudden appearance of 4-word phrases with altered beats are completely not related to the rest of the piece.

5. Rhythm
No strong impression of directions at all. Each bar isn't emphasised. It's as if the whole piece is running flat.

6. Dynamics
Absolutely nothing.

7. Orchestration
The text has a lot of "outcry", "sounding thoroughly" and "sound of trumpet", but the brass isn't really playing a strong role. The "harmonic" parts are also only with the middle and lower registers. The characteristics of each instrument haven't been used idiomatically. There are a few hits of the snare drum, but no one uses the snare drum like this sneakily. If we must give more strength on the lower register, we may use the Trombone to play longer notes, or use the Cello and Double Bass to play repeated short notes or even tremolo. Using the whole orchestra to play chordals, that's not very productive.

8. Functional Harmony
This piece of music doesn't have other musical languages at all. He has chosen Functional Harmony, but the most boring kind. Anyway, the question is, is it used with enough tension and release? Nope. Every line almost runs the same: IV-V-I, no change. Every line begins with the Subdominant IV, going into Dominant V and then back to Tonic I. This is almost like the kind of "by-the-book" functional harmony. It's only used when you really want to deliberately make it boring. If you think social movements should be like water, why not try pan-diatonicism? Or even something similar to traditional Cantonese music with Pentatonic scale with modal harmony?

And I also don't understand why it is written in Major, yet the melody is always going downwards, alongside deep and gloomy text and theme.

When we talk about disappointment or dispair, we may use the Minor Key. The Subdominant iv into Tonic i (both minor chords) or even Subdominant iv -> Leading diminished vii° -> Tonic i. These progressions at least have a strong push towards the ending resolution. When nearing the end of the song, when we finally see hope, we may change it back into the Major Key, and we may use the Deceptive Cadence I -> IV -> V -> iv, and then back into your Perfect Cadence.

9. Modulation
No change of Key at all. When your piece of music is clearly divided into 2 parts with very different themes, it is best written with 2 different keys.

10. Cantonese tone matching the melody's pitches
Fine.

Summary: (1/10), only 1 out of 10 has been done with efforts.

From Number 1 through 9 are all low efforts. This kind of music is usually scraped as the first version. You will feel embarrassed if you bring this to your composition teacher.

A composer is very intimate to your own piece of music. A composer has to spend a lot of time communicating with his own composition. You have to spend time and effort to improve your work. So if the composer of this piece has so many friends in LiHKG, then he should at least ask for advices, and go back-and-forth for at least 4 to 5 versions to slowly improve it. After having slowly refined it in a much better state, should one release it into the public and ask people to perform it.

This piece of music has potential. But it lacks efforts. It's almost like the composer was rushing to hand in an assignment. The composer hasn't spent much time fine-tuning the piece of music. And it doesn't seem like the musical experts of LiHKG has been giving him enough feedback for improvements. It is something simply done in a rush with cheap and low efforts.

As a musicologist, I also have my own criticisms of this song, however considering the short turnaround time, the constraints of having to incorporate so many lyrical ideas and elements yet needing a coherent structure, I think this song serves as a great song for our current time. Sure it can be better, sure it's rather difficult to sing, but is it better than not having a song of our own? I think this song is the song we need for the time we need it. But your critique is appreciated and we need people like you to continue to improve our craft!

樂評《願榮光歸香港》
Criticism in Music - Glory to Hong Kong
(English below)

上次簡略介紹了一些可用的工具來作批評文學，現在當然是真係寫作一篇批評文學了。我們做的是文化工作，當然要在文化實力上把關。

1. 歌詞
這歌詞和我們日常談話的語言是很不同的，也沒有什麼詩意。歌詞開初使用了很多反問，而反問句基本上全部都沒有回答，這是近似於控訴式的傷痕文學字詞。但這種傷痕卻又不是很深的傷痕，近似於撞傷撞瘀，而不是流血如注。沒呼天搶地，只道悲暗愁煩，也沒有表達什麼希望，近似於以死控訴的心境。

2. 旋律
這絕對不是如歌的旋律，每一句句子都很難做出什麼連貫句，每一句也像斬件式的，一塊叉燒斬開三件的模樣。

如果是先曲後詞，這曲一開始便將人說話的句子分拆開，每一下也像是「擢一擢」的感覺，有如開船進大海，不斷被海浪打斷氣息。這樣的音樂是不論填上什麼語言也會令人覺得難唱。

如果是先詞後曲，這詞寫得也很奇怪。唐詩宋詞也沒有以兩三字為一句，標點符號佔了全文三分一篇幅的寫法。

3. 旋律與歌詞的配合
基本上歌詞和旋律是沒有關連的。「淚再流」ti-do-la，「亦憤恨」mi-fa-mi，都是停在穩定而大調色彩豐富的地方；再下句「拒沉默」居然是高音的 fa-mi-re，而「吶喊聲響透」是 mi-fa-sol-mi-do 向下走。基本上旋律的方向和文字是幾乎相反。

而如果你真的要說這是軍歌式風格的，至少旋律不要向下走，不要每一句句尾都是向下的。

4. 拍子
基本上由一開始到後半首的忽然變動為止，都是一個基本的拍子：附點一拍、半拍、附點一拍、半拍。完全沒有變化。然後忽然出現了一些四字詞用了很忽然出現的東西，是和整首音樂沒有主題連貫的。

5. 節奏
每一個小節的正拍沒有什麼強烈的感覺，沒有造出方向感。每一句也是平伏的進行。

6. 大細聲變化
完全沒有。

7. 配器法
歌詞寫了很多的「吶喊」、「響透」、「號角聲」，但基本上銅管沒有一個明顯的角色。用作演奏和聲的部份也只是有中低音域，沒有就每一件樂器的特色來給予他們不同的音域來突顯優勢。背後偶然聽到的小鼓聲也是不明所以，沒有人用小鼓得來又用得這麼鬼祟的。要在和聲低聲部增加強度的話，其實是適合使用多一點的長號吹長音，或者使用大提琴和低音大提琴拉奏短促和重覆的音，而不是要求全隊樂團以單粒單粒音的形式去「墊底」。

8. 功能和聲
這音樂沒有其他的音樂語言，他是選擇了功性和聲，而且是最老土的一款。但不要緊，問題是，使用得是否鬆張有度？不，基本上每一句也是同一功能：IV-V-I，沒有變化。每一句都以IV開首，轉入V，再回來I。這種... 是刻意的「按章式」功能和聲。是刻意製造沉悶的感覺才會用的。其實當你認為社運應該如流水，為何不找泛調性來用用？或者是近似於傳統粵樂中的五聲音階的調式音樂？

而且我不明白為何要使用大調得來，旋律是向下，而且是用深沉的歌詞和音樂內容。

其實表達失望或「墜落徬徨」時，可以使用小調，主力使用 iv -> i ，甚至是 iv -> vii° -> i 這種強烈的推進。當臨尾談到希望是，再轉回大調，使用 I -> IV -> V -> vi 再 I -> IV -> V -> I 等。

9. 轉調
完全無轉調。其實你隻歌明顯係分開兩段，有不同的主題、有不同的拍子、有不同的內容，是應該使用不同的調。

10. 填詞合音
還算可以。

總結 (1/10)，十項中只有一項是花過精神和心血的。

第1至9項也是下苦功不足，基本上這種作品是起稿的第一版本，多數拿到作曲課的老師面前也令人自己害羞的。

作曲的人，和自己所作的曲，是很親切和微妙的關係。你作為一個作曲家，你要和自己的音樂好好相處一下，要花點時間心血去改進。作好出來，你既然有連登的朋友，就應該好好請教一下他們，來回至少三五個版本，再慢慢改進，做得至少合符一個作曲家的心血，才會放出來教人演奏傳播。

這首音樂，是有潛質的，可惜完全沒有雕琢、沒有花心血去改進，像是老師要求交功課便隨便完成交貨似的，作曲家沒有和音樂相處得很久似的，也見不到有向他的連登網友們發問，是一種低廉、低努力的創作。
